questions.questionText:
O is the centre of a circle. A and B are points on the circumference.
The angle AOB = 112°.
Find the angle ACB where C is another point on the major arc.

markScheme.desc: Angle at centre = 2 × angle at circumference

workingSteps: Angle at centre = 2 × angle at circumference (same arc)
questions.explanation: The angle at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference subtended by the same arc.

questions.questionText:
ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral.
Angle ABC = 108° and angle BCD = 75°.
Find angles ADC and DAB, giving reasons.

markScheme.desc: Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°

workingSteps: Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary (sum to 180°)
questions.explanation: Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral add up to 180°.

questions.context:
O is the centre of a circle. T is a point outside the circle.
TA and TB are tangents from T to the circle.
OA = 5 cm and TA = 12 cm.

parts.label: a
parts.correctAnswer: The tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.

markScheme.desc: Tangent is perpendicular to radius at point of contact oe

workingSteps: A tangent meets the radius at right angles at the point of tangency.

parts.label: b
workingSteps: Triangle OAT is right-angled at A

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Write down the length TB, giving a reason.

markScheme.desc: Tangents from an external point are equal in length oe

workingSteps: Tangents drawn from an external point are equal in length

parts.label: d

markScheme.desc: cos(angle AOT) = OA/OT = 5/13, so angle AOT = 67.38...

workingSteps: In right triangle OAT: cos(AOT) = OA/OT = 5/13
